title: The Bedtime Signal Ritual
subtitle: A five-part ritual for building a consistent sleep signal through nightly routine
excerpt: A nighttime ritual from Day 25 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The practice has five steps: Prepare, Release, Allow, Integrate, and Anchor. Together they establish a deliberate sequence of small cues that tell the nervous system the day is over and sleep is safe, a nightly permission slip built from repetition.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 25 night practice of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795). The release-and-allow structure draws from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

content.text: A five-step ritual for the transition into sleep. The nervous system learns through repeated signals that a particular sequence of steps means 'safe to stop'. This ritual builds that signal deliberately (a cup of tea, a few slow taps, lowered lighting), small cues strung together into a consistent nightly bridge.

Lower the lighting in the room. Make a warm drink if that's part of your routine. It doesn't need to be. Sit somewhere comfortable. The specific sequence you choose matters less than doing it the same way each night.
I am ready to release the day: every task, every unfinished thing, every thought still running. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align with and allow rest: deep, full, uninterrupted. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Take one slow breath in and all the way out. Picture the room as quiet and complete. Nothing is waiting for you tonight. The door between day and night is closed.
Say this once, quietly, as you move toward bed:
My bedtime routine is my signal. My body knows what comes next.
Adapted from the Day 25 night practice of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The release-and-allow structure draws from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
